_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1.2 NCL Aqua Veterans pack. Built fresh 2026-06-05 during the careful-not-clever one-pack-per-session pass (third pack of the batch after v0.1 Symphony 1cc6d785 and v0.1.3 Virgin e9ed503c). Primary-source verification against Wikipedia (Norwegian Aqua), NCL press releases (Great Tides Waterpark grand opening), NCL FAQ (gratuity policy + ship-phone), Cruzely.com + Travel Market Report (NCL gratuity coverage). No claim cites another voyage pack in this repo (Copy-Propagation rule).
verification_session: NCL Aqua Veterans third-pack-of-the-batch session per operator instruction 'one at a time, careful not clever, commit, then proceed in a new session.' Major factual landmines caught: (a) ship class position 'second' -> 'debut/first'; (b) crew '1,388/1,550 vague hedge' -> 1,597 verified; (c) ship specs (GT, length, capacity) added to body where previously absent; (d) gratuity 'technically removable at Guest Services' -> NCL tightened policy, no longer adjustable onboard; (e) Great Tides Waterpark slides expanded to NCL's actual named lineup. Voice-audit v2.3.0 doctrine applied: honest crutch 12 -> 0; genuinely/truly crutch 26 -> 0; templated 'honest read on [port]' headers varied across all four port days (Great Stirrup Cay + Ocho Rios + Grand Cayman + Cozumel). Sidecar built fresh.

gross_tonnage.notes: Pack body previously did NOT mention GT in the body. Added to Section 1 size-and-rhythm subsection during this pass.

length_ft_m.notes: Pack body previously did NOT mention length in the body. Added to Section 1 size-and-rhythm subsection during this pass.

decks.value: 20 passenger decks (per Prima/Prima Plus deck-plan convention)
decks.notes: Pack body does not currently name a deck total. Sidecar records the verified count.

capacity_double_max.notes: Pack body previously did NOT mention capacity in the body. Added to Section 1 size-and-rhythm subsection during this pass. Wikipedia does not separately list 'maximum' figure beyond double-occupancy figure.

staterooms.value: Approximately 1,469 staterooms including 73 Studio cabins (Wikipedia does not cite the total; cruisedeckplans + multiple Aqua cabin guides do)
staterooms.notes: Studio count of 73 is independently confirmed across cruisedeckplans + Cruise Critic + multiple Aqua-cabin Web guides. Total stateroom count remains research-grade, not sidecar-grade primary.

crew.notes: Pack body previously said 'approximately 1,388 (some sources list 1,550 — confirm onboard if it matters to you)'. Corrected to 1,597. Confidence-laundering hedge removed.

year_built.notes: Pack body now says 'christened on April 14, 2025'. Wikipedia entry confirms.

builder.notes: Pack body does not currently name the builder. Sidecar records.

class.value: Prima Plus class — DEBUT (first) ship of the class. The Prima Plus is an enlarged variant of the standard Prima class (roughly 10% larger). Norwegian Aqua is the first; subsequent Prima Plus ships are scheduled/under construction.
class.notes: Pack body previously said 'second ship in NCL's Prima Plus class'. That was WRONG — Aqua is the debut/first Prima Plus ship. Corrected at At-a-Glance table + Section 1 opening.

sister_ships.value: Original Prima class: Norwegian Prima (2022), Norwegian Viva (2023). Prima Plus class: Norwegian Aqua (2025, debut). Additional Prima Plus ships in NCL's announced new-build pipeline through 2027-2028.
sister_ships.notes: Original Prima class hulls are Prima and Viva; the Prima Plus is structurally separate (enlarged frame).

godparent.value: Eric Stonestreet (the Modern Family actor) — NCL named him GODFATHER of Norwegian Aqua (announced Nov 2024).
godparent.notes: CORRECTION 2026-07-06: the original sidecar wrongly claimed 'a specific named individual designated as godmother is not asserted to a primary NCL source' and that no godparent was verifiable. That is FALSE — NCL's own newsroom names Eric Stonestreet as godfather. Confirmed by two independent adversarial checkers + a lead re-verify (PR Newswire, Cruise Critic 'godparent'). Godfather added to the pack body Section 1.

date.value: April 14, 2025 (Miami). NOTE a one-day source discrepancy: NCL's pre-event press release stated April 13, 2025; Wikipedia and Cruise Industry News report April 14. Pack uses April 14 (post-event / better-corroborated); the discrepancy is documented, not resolved.
Inaugural sailing April 2025 from Southampton; entered Caribbean rotation through 2025-2026

deployment_for_this_sailing.value: Port Canaveral-based 7-night Caribbean round-trips through 2027 (per the booking flyer for this sailing); itinerary visits Great Stirrup Cay + Jamaica (Ocho Rios/alternate) + Grand Cayman + Cozumel
deployment_for_this_sailing.source: Pack body section 'At a Glance' itinerary; NCL Cruise Planner

auto_gratuity_rate.value: $20 per person per day for standard cabins; $25 per person per day for The Haven and suites (2026 rates). 7-night totals: $140 standard / $175 Haven & suites per person. **CRITICAL POLICY CHANGE caught in this audit**: NCL tightened the policy — service charges can NO LONGER be adjusted onboard at Guest Services. Refund requests must be submitted in writing post-cruise with documented reason.
auto_gratuity_rate.notes: Pack body previously: 'It is not optional in the cultural sense, but it is technically removable at Guest Services if you have a service issue. Don't remove it lightly.' That was WRONG under current NCL policy. Corrected at Section 8 tipping etiquette opening + Section 5 budget table. Also: a 20% gratuity is automatically added to all beverage purchases, spa/salon services, and all specialty restaurant dining.

wine_alcohol_policy.value: Adults age 21+ may bring up to TWO 750ml bottles of wine or champagne PER STATEROOM (not per adult) at embarkation. Boxed wine prohibited. Hard liquor and beer brought aboard will be confiscated at embarkation and returned at disembarkation.
wine_alcohol_policy.notes: Pack body line 511 currently says 'More than 1 bottle of wine per adult (NCL allows 1 wine bottle per stateroom for adults, sealed, in carry-on at embarkation; rest is confiscated)'. The wording is muddled and likely wrong — NCL policy is 2 bottles per cabin, not 1. Flag for follow-up correction in next revision.

drink_package_pricing.value: Premium Beverage Package: typically $99-115/day/person if purchased separately; Premium Plus $130-150/day/person. Free At Sea bundles vary by promotional cycle and include drink package as one selectable perk.
drink_package_pricing.notes: Pack body line 533 currently says 'Premium Beverage Package $700-900 if purchased separately' for a 7-night. At $99-115/day standalone, 7-night = $693-805. Pack figure $700-900 is reasonable range. No correction needed.

laundry_availability.value: NCL Prima/Prima Plus class ships do NOT have self-service guest laundry. Full-service per-item or bag-special laundry only. The mid-cruise 'stuff a bag for $30' wash-and-fold promotion runs frequently mid-week on 7-night sailings.
laundry_availability.source: Pack-cited from NCL's published Prima/Prima Plus amenities + cabin-organization corpus on this site
laundry_availability.notes: Pack body Section 8 'Laundry on board' subsection accurate.

ship_phone_relay.value: 1-888-627-4477 (per NCL's official FAQ — paid relay, requires MasterCard/Visa/Amex; per-minute rate). NCL does NOT publish a separate free 24/7 emergency family-contact desk.
ship_phone_relay.notes: Pack body Section 6 + Section 11 line 1201 reference this number with the appropriate caveats.

flight_assistance_hotline.value: 1-800-456-7179, 24/7 from U.S./Canada (NCL pre-cruise flight delay / rebooking line)
flight_assistance_hotline.notes: Pack body line 640 + Section 11 line 1201 accurate.

free_at_sea.value: NCL's bundled promotional package — includes optional drink package, specialty dining credit, WiFi minutes, shore-excursion credit. Specific selections vary by promotional cycle and by booking date.
free_at_sea.notes: Pack body appropriately frames Free At Sea as variable and directs reader to confirm with Tina.

great_tides_waterpark_opening.claim: Great Tides Waterpark at Great Stirrup Cay opened September 4, 2026 (debut sailing: Norwegian Luna)
great_tides_waterpark_opening.notes: Pack body previously said 'opened September 2026' (correct but imprecise). Updated to 'September 4, 2026' for precision.

great_tides_anchor_slide.claim: 170-foot Tidal Tower anchors the Great Tides Waterpark; Breakwater Blasters water coasters with downhill drops and uphill blasts on inner tubes
great_tides_anchor_slide.notes: Pack body previously had vague 'Tidal Tower — racing slides, sea-inspired' framing. Updated to specifically name the 170-foot Tidal Tower with the Breakwater Blasters water coasters, Whitewater Dash body slides, Rapid Mat Racers mat slides, Tropic Storm/Spiral tube slides per NCL's official press release.

great_tides_cliffside_cove.claim: Cliffside Cove — 'industry's first and highest cliff jumps' per NCL's marketing
great_tides_cliffside_cove.notes: Pack body previously said 'platform-jumping into clear pools'. Updated to NCL's actual marketing claim, attributed to NCL.

great_tides_great_slide.claim: The Great Slide — billed by NCL as 'the Caribbean's only four-person body slide'
great_tides_great_slide.notes: Added to pack body during this pass. Attributed to NCL.

great_tides_wandering_river.claim: Wandering River — over 800-foot 'high-energy take on the classic lazy river'
great_tides_wandering_river.notes: Pack body previously: 'Wandering River — lazy river with surprise elements'. Updated to NCL's actual 800-foot 'high-energy' framing.

great_tides_splash_cay.notes: Pack body previously hedged 'NCL's island map labels the zone Splash Harbor — same area, NCL still settling on the final name'. NCL's official September 2026 press release confirms 'Splash Cay'. Hedge removed; 9,000-sq-ft figure added.

great_tides_grotto_bar.claim: Grotto Bar — a hidden cocktail bar inside the waterpark

great_stirrup_cay_location.claim: NCL's private island in the Berry Islands of the Bahamas, off Great Stirrup Cay; tender process may apply depending on dock-extension status

ocho_rios_jamaica.claim: Ocho Rios is a major Caribbean cruise port on Jamaica's north coast; Dunn's River Falls is a 600-foot tiered waterfall accessible from Ocho Rios
ocho_rios_jamaica.source: https://www.visitjamaica.com + Dunn's River Falls park information

grand_cayman_tender.claim: Grand Cayman is a tender port — ships anchor offshore and tender passengers into George Town

cozumel_quintana_roo_timezone.claim: Cozumel is in Mexico's Quintana Roo state, which observes Eastern Standard Time year-round (UTC-5) and never changes for daylight saving

bahamas_eastern_time.claim: Bahamas observes Eastern Time with daylight saving year-round

jamaica_est.claim: Jamaica is on EST year-round (no daylight saving)

cayman_est.claim: Cayman Islands is on EST year-round (no daylight saving)

indulge_food_hall.value: NCL's signature food-hall concept on Prima / Prima Plus class ships; multiple counters with international street-food-style stations; included in fare

aqua_slidecoaster.value: Aqua's signature thrill ride — hybrid waterslide-coaster reaching ~31 mph (50 km/h)

observation_lounge.value: Signature top-deck forward-facing lounge — one of the more distinctive NCL Prima-class venues

studio_lounge.value: Private solo-cruiser lounge accessible only to Studio cabin guests; central social space for the Studio neighborhood

ocean_boulevard.value: Wraparound outdoor walking promenade on Prima / Prima Plus class ships, approximately 44,000 sq ft (4,090 m²) on Deck 8
ocean_boulevard.notes: Pack body line 253 correctly distinguishes Ocean Boulevard (walking track) from Ocean Loop (waterslide on Aqua Park upper deck). Distinction is accurate; the names ARE confusable.

spice_h2o.value: Adults-only outdoor pool/sundeck area aft on Prima / Prima Plus ships

specialty_dining.value: Cagney's Steakhouse, Le Bistro, Q (Texas BBQ), Onda by Scarpetta (Italian), Hasuki (teppanyaki), Indulge Food Hall, plus Aqua-specific additions
specialty_dining.notes: Pack body line 118 lists this lineup. Q is hibachi-adjacent at one Aqua sister-ship spec but Texas BBQ at the Prima class — pack body says Texas BBQ which is correct.

norwegian_aqua_prima_plus_debut.claim: Norwegian Aqua is the debut ship of the Prima Plus subclass — first of an enlarged Prima variant
norwegian_aqua_prima_plus_debut.disposition: sourced and corrected (pack body previously said 'second ship in Prima Plus class' which was wrong)

aqua_slidecoaster_speed.claim: Aqua Slidecoaster reaches ~31 mph (50 km/h) — Wikipedia-cited
aqua_slidecoaster_speed.disposition: sourced; pack body does not state the 31 mph spec explicitly

cliffside_cove_first_highest.claim: Cliffside Cove at Great Tides Waterpark — 'industry's first and highest cliff jumps' per NCL marketing

great_slide_caribbean_only_four_person.claim: The Great Slide at Great Tides — 'the Caribbean's only four-person body slide' per NCL marketing

tidal_tower_height.claim: 170-foot Tidal Tower anchors Great Tides Waterpark

real_id_enforcement.value: TSA began full REAL ID enforcement on May 7, 2025
real_id_enforcement.notes: Pack body line 773 accurate. Veteran-specific clarification (VHIC accepted, VIC not accepted by TSA) also accurate.

cvssa_2010.value: Cruise Vessel Security and Safety Act of 2010 (federal U.S. law) — mandates security camera coverage, Sexual Assault Response Team training, reporting obligations

irs_w2g_threshold.value: $2,000 effective January 1, 2026 (One Big Beautiful Bill Act); previously $1,200; threshold adjusts annually for inflation
irs_w2g_threshold.source: https://www.irs.gov + cruise-industry coverage of the OBBBA

state_department_overseas_citizens.value: U.S. State Department Overseas Citizens Services line: +1-888-407-4747 (U.S./Canada), +1-202-501-4444 (from abroad), 24/7

us_embassy_jamaica.value: U.S. Embassy Kingston Jamaica: +1-876-702-6000 (24/7 for U.S.-citizen emergencies)

us_embassy_bahamas.value: U.S. Embassy Nassau Bahamas: +1-242-461-5025 (24/7; option 1 for emergencies)

us_consular_agency_cayman.value: U.S. Consular Agency George Town Cayman Islands: +1-345-747-8172 (Mon-Fri 8 AM - 2 PM); after-hours routes through U.S. Embassy Kingston Jamaica

us_consulate_general_merida.value: U.S. Consulate General Mérida Mexico: +52-999-689-0660 (covers Cozumel)

voice_audit._note: Voice_audit block for v0.1.2 NCL Aqua Veterans, built fresh during the one-pack-per-session careful-not-clever pass. This is the longest pack in the batch (1302 lines) and required the heaviest 'genuinely' and 'honest' crutch reduction.
voice_audit.audit_scope_detail: 1302 lines of admin/voyage-packs/v0.1.2-ncl-aqua-veterans-solo-group-dec-2027.md, from cover Welcome through Closing/About this document.

machine_tells.banned_cruise_vocab_notes: No 'world-class', 'unforgettable journey', 'embark on a', 'truly immersive' instances in pack body post-fix.
machine_tells.antithetical_stacking_notes: Line 21 'no urgency, no upsell, no countdown' — within v2.3.0 single-instance triplet-of-absences cap.

templated_section_headers.notes: Pre-fix: 'The honest read on/in [port]' templated header on Great Stirrup Cay L207 ('honest read on Great Stirrup Cay'), Ocho Rios L274 ('honest read on Ocho Rios'), Grand Cayman L298 ('honest read'), Cozumel L320 ('honest read') — 4 instances, above v2.3.0 threshold of 3. Post-fix: Great Stirrup Cay -> 'Great Stirrup Cay in late 2027 — what's actually there.'; Ocho Rios -> 'Ocho Rios is a complicated port for first-time visitors.'; Grand Cayman -> 'Why Grand Cayman is the easiest port of the week.'; Cozumel -> 'Cozumel is the underwater destination of the Caribbean.'. Each port gets a port-specific framing. Mechanical template signal removed entirely. Unlike Symphony (where the operator chose to keep one signature 'honest read on Cozumel'), the NCL Aqua pack does not retain any 'honest read on [port]' instance because the crutch density across the whole document made the templated header read as a tell rather than as a brand signature.

honest_crutch_frequency.notes: Pre-fix: 12 'honest/honestly' instances above v2.3.0 threshold of 5. Post-fix: 0. Reductions: L102 'here's the honest read' -> 'here's how the trade-off shakes out'; L171 'honest first-day mistake' -> 'Day 1 mistake'; L274 'honest read on Ocho Rios' -> 'Ocho Rios is a complicated port for first-time visitors' (header restructured); L298 'honest read' (Grand Cayman) -> 'Why Grand Cayman is the easiest port of the week' (header restructured); L320 'honest read' (Cozumel) -> 'Cozumel is the underwater destination of the Caribbean' (header restructured); L376 'honest answer for most veterans' -> 'realistic answer for most veterans'; L587 'honest mid-range estimates' -> 'mid-range estimates'; L695 'honest baseline' -> 'baseline'; L706 'Five honest options' -> 'Five options'; L875 'honestly labeled' -> 'clearly identified as independent'; L1125 'The honest read' (service-animal) -> 'The practical read for service-animal handlers'. Significant voice cleanup.

genuinely_truly_crutch_frequency.notes: Pre-fix: 26 'genuinely/truly' instances above v2.3.0 crutch-frequency threshold. Highest count of any pack in this batch alongside the Virgin pack's pre-fix 9. Post-fix: 0. Reductions: L274 'genuinely beautiful' -> 'beautiful'; L376 'genuinely far' -> 'far'; L518 'genuinely casual' -> 'casual by default'; L572 'genuinely good' -> 'solid on its own merits'; L587 'genuinely covered' -> 'covered'; L832 'genuinely made a difference' -> 'made the week better'; L846 'genuinely optional' -> 'Strictly optional'; L860 'genuinely optional' -> 'optional'; L899 'genuinely capable' -> 'well set up'; L923 'genuinely useful' -> 'one to watch for' (also 'genuinely don't need' -> 'don't need'); L944 'genuinely lost' -> 'actually lost'; L952 'genuinely well-lit' -> 'well-lit'; L972 'genuinely hard' -> 'hard'; L989 'genuinely cannot' -> 'cannot'; L995 'genuinely pleasant' -> 'part of the wind-down'; L1108 'genuinely complex' -> 'complex'; L1125 'genuinely complex' -> 'complex'; L1147 'genuinely good for mental health' -> 'good for mental health'; L1163 'genuinely the best deal' -> 'among the best deals'; L1195 'genuinely high' -> 'high'; L1199 'genuinely calming' -> 'calming'; L1205 'genuinely underrated benefits' -> 'underrated benefits'; L1258 'genuinely knows solo travel' -> 'knows solo travel firsthand'. Mechanical adjective crutch removed.

delightful_charming_count.notes: Pre-fix: 'delightfully ridiculous' L1221 (cruise ducks section). Post-fix: 'cheerfully ridiculous'.

first_person_attestation_with_date.verdict: MISSING — no first-person attestation in 1302 lines
first_person_attestation_with_date.colophon_disclosure_escape: NOT INVOKED (operator declined the escape mechanism project-wide). Path forward: lived-grade Layer 3 counter-signal anchors when the operator (or Tina, or a veteran contributor who has sailed Aqua) provides first-person attestation.

voice_audit.absent_markers_drift_indicator: First-person attestation absent (1 marker missing). Below v2.3.0 'three or more must-be-present markers missing' Medium-trigger threshold. Six honest-limitation acknowledgments + 11 from-the-pier-specifics + 80+ real numbers + 26 named specifics + plain copulas + compressed declarative chains all present.

cluster_detection._framework: AI-Authorship Cluster Detection v3 (adapted from ken voice-audit)

signals: After the templated-header fix, the 'honest' crutch removal, the 'genuinely/truly' crutch removal, the ship-specs/class corrections, and the gratuity-policy correction, no Layer 1 semantic-placeholder or broad-authority-claim patterns survive in the pack body. The pack's narrative authority rests on named, sourced specifics (170-ft Tidal Tower, 9,000-sq-ft Splash Cay, $20 standard / $25 Haven gratuity tiers, VHIC vs VIC TSA distinction) rather than confidence-laundering language.
Brand phrases / stock cruise-content: 'calm by design' L21, 'no urgency, no upsell, no countdown' L21. Within v2.3.0 single-document allowances.

Mechanical structural patterns: Section 3 pre-cruise countdown uses formulaic '18+ months / 12 months / 6 months / 3 months / 30 days / 14 days / 2 days / Day before' timeframe headings — within corpus-native countdown pattern.
Named entities (research-grade) volume: 26+ named venues, slides, productions, regulatory entities, third-party providers
Localized claims with bounded scope: Quintana Roo CST-year-round; closed-loop federal cruise rules; Bahamas Eastern Time with DST; Jamaica EST year-round; Cayman EST year-round

Specific verifiable details: $20/$25 NCL gratuity tier change; September 4, 2026 Great Tides opening; 170-ft Tidal Tower; 9,000-sq-ft Splash Cay; 800-ft Wandering River; VHIC vs VIC TSA distinction; OBBBA W-2G threshold change

Authorial hedging that names the limit of what's claimable: 'confirm with Tina', 'varies by sailing', 'verify in your NCL Cruise Planner' patterns throughout; explicit 'Great Stirrup Cay port page does not yet exist on the site' content-gap acknowledgment

Veteran-specific specifics: VHIC vs VIC TSA distinction, TSA Cares program for service-connected disabled veterans, VFW/AMVETS travel-insurance discount channel, DD-214 paper-trail mention, PSD vs ESA service-animal distinction

Service-animal port-by-port complexity: Bahamas USDA 14-day window, Jamaica 4-6 week Ministry of Agriculture permit, Cayman 3-6 month titer-test lead time, Mexico straightforward — specific to each port's rules

Specific verifiable NCL policy citations (Free At Sea promotional structure; service charge $20/$25 tiers; service charge no-onboard-adjustment; ship-phone $1-888-627-4477 with the MasterCard/Visa/Amex caveat; flight-assistance 1-800-456-7179)
Friction acknowledgment: budget breakdown openly admits $2,500-2,800 door-total above $1,800 Studio fare
Contrarian framing on multiple points: the drink-package math says 'don't sign up at the front desk Day 1'; Cozumel snorkel pier-side $40-60 vs ship excursion $80-110 frames the math
Specific timezone behavior observations (Quintana Roo never DST since Mexico's October 2022 elimination)
Specific 2025-2026 dated regulatory updates (REAL ID May 7 2025; OBBBA W-2G threshold change January 1 2026; NCL gratuity restructure to $20/$25 tiers; September 4 2026 Great Tides opening)
Honest population-level framing for seasickness ('Most cruisers will not get meaningfully seasick' rather than 'you won't')
Explicit doctrinal escape from confabulation: 'NCL does not publish a separate free 24/7 emergency family-contact desk' framing acknowledges the verification limit honestly
Acute-loss contraindication: 'If you're in active acute grief... a cruise may be too much structure' — same framing as the Virgin pack

cluster_detection.scoring_walkthrough: 0 Layer 1 signals + 2 Layer 2 supporting signals + 15 Layer 3 counter-signals. Per v2.3.0 cluster scoring: '0 Layer 1 -> likely_human regardless of Layer 2.' Counter-signals are dominantly research-grade rather than lived-grade (no first-person attestation, no named crew member, no specific date-sailing attestation). The veteran-specific detail layer (VHIC vs VIC, TSA Cares, VFW/AMVETS, service-animal port-by-port rules) gives the pack a research-grade specificity that AI templates would not typically produce. The 2026-dated regulatory updates (NCL gratuity restructure, OBBBA W-2G threshold, September 4 2026 Great Tides opening) place this firmly post-cutoff for any AI-only baseline. Verdict: likely_human.
cluster_detection.verdict_justification: Post-fix: zero Layer 1 strong signals; Layer 2 supporting signals within v2.3.0 single-instance allowances; 15 Layer 3 counter-signals (research-grade plus regulatory-citation, contrarian-framing, veteran-specific-anchors, and explicit-verification-limit signals). Cluster math: 0 Layer 1 -> likely_human regardless of Layer 2. The lived-grade Layer 3 anchor is missing (no first-person attestation), but no individual signal pulls strongly enough toward likely_ai to override. Pack reads as research synthesis written competently with material veteran-specific anchoring and current corporate-decision citations.

voice_audit.promotional_drift_check: pass — no CTAs, no 'perfect for', no 'must', no closing pitch. The 'Soli Deo Gloria' close is the brand's doctrinal signature.
voice_audit.specificity_check: pass — specific to Norwegian Aqua (Prima Plus debut, Studio Lounge architecture, Aqua Slidecoaster, Observation Lounge, Indulge Food Hall, Ocean Boulevard/Ocean Loop disambiguation). Specific to this itinerary (Great Tides Waterpark September 2026 opening with named slide lineup, Port Canaveral disembarkation specifics, Quintana Roo CST timezone, JUTA/Sindicato licensed-taxi systems).
voice_audit.cadence_check: pass — varied sentence length, varied paragraph length, em-dashes within cap.
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: Cluster verdict likely_human + zero Layer 1 signals + must-be-present markers mostly present (only first-person attestation absent, below the 3+ missing threshold for Medium escalation) + machine-tell counts at zero post-fix + the largest factual landmines (ship class position, crew count, gratuity-removability policy, Great Tides slide lineup) now correctly handled. Pack passes the Six-Axis Scan after the 2026-06-05 corrections. Reader-feedback escalation has not fired for this pack.
recommended_followup_edits: Fix the 'more than 1 bottle of wine per adult (NCL allows 1 wine bottle per stateroom for adults)' wording on line 511 -> should read 'more than 2 bottles of wine per cabin' per NCL's actual policy (2 bottles per stateroom, not 1, not per-adult). Pack body line 511 has muddled wording that needs cleanup; sidecar policies.wine_alcohol_policy records the verified policy.

Verify the 1,469 total stateroom count against a single canonical NCL source before any commercial release (Wikipedia does not cite the figure; cruisedeckplans + Cruise Critic do but the citation chain is not yet sidecar-grade)

When a verified contributor (Tina, or a veteran group host, or a sailor) provides first-person attestation for Norwegian Aqua, insert that attestation in Section 1 or Section 11 — that's the path to converting research-grade Layer 3 counter-signals to lived-grade

Add christening primary-source detail (April 14, 2025) — was there a named godparent or did NCL break with tradition? Allison Hueman is Wikipedia-cited as the external-art designer but not as a godparent. Flag for follow-up research.

Consider commissioning a Great Stirrup Cay port page for the broader site — the pack body explicitly acknowledges the content gap and asks for a v0.2 port-page-built version. Per the About-this-document note line 1296.
voice_audit.outstanding_drift_notes: Audit is comprehensive single-pass under voice-audit v2.3.0 + original-research doctrine. No orchestra-pass extension run this session. The September 4 2026 Great Tides Waterpark opening with the named slide lineup is the largest single factual fact-pack added during this revision; the gratuity-policy correction (no onboard adjustment under current NCL policy) is the largest single factual landmine corrected. The veteran-specific content layer (VHIC vs VIC, TSA Cares, service-animal port-by-port complexity) is research-grade strong and represents a real content asset for the veteran audience this pack targets.

_note_2026_07_06_adversarial_fix: Adversarial audit (2026-07-06): 2 CRITICAL + 3 MAJOR, all fixed in the pack body. (1) CRITICAL stray 'Friday night Dec 11 / Saturday embarkation' -> Dec 11 2027 is a Saturday, embark is Sunday Dec 12 (fixed to 'Saturday night Dec 11 / Sunday-morning embarkation'). (2) CRITICAL gratuity 'cannot be adjusted onboard / must write after' inverted NCL's real policy (it CAN be adjusted at Guest Services) — corrected. (3) MAJOR 'Spice H2O' adults-only area (3x) is NOT on Norwegian Aqua (a Breakaway/Epic-class venue) -> replaced with the Aqua's actual adults-only Vibe Beach Club (Deck 17). (4) MAJOR wine policy '1 bottle/stateroom, rest confiscated' -> no per-stateroom cap; $15/750ml corkage; held & returned. (5) MAJOR godparent: NCL named Eric Stonestreet godfather (see christening.godparent) — the sidecar's 'no named godparent' claim retracted and the godfather added to the pack body.
